title: Count the Carrion Columns
flavor: A pale legate unrolls a map etched in fingerbone. The Bone Court pays for eyes: number the horde's columns moving north of {ridge}, and say nothing of who asked.

title: The Pale Legate's Road
flavor: An envoy of the Bone Court must cross {road} unmolested. The Court dislikes surprises and pays for their absence.

title: Silence the Signal Fires
flavor: The horde talks to itself with fire along {ridge}. The Court would prefer silence. Ride fast, burn the beacons, be gone before the smoke settles.

title: Bone Meal and Black Glass
flavor: Forty crates of reagents to {gate}, unopened. The Court is precise about 'unopened.' Dull work; clean coin.

title: Burn the Shambling Vanguard
flavor: A forward camp of the horde festers within a day's march. The Court wants it ash by week's end — every walking thing in it unmade.

title: Hold the Glass Bridge
flavor: The horde will push at {bridge} before the black moon. The Court requires the crossing held. It does not require survivors — but it pays better for them.

title: Unmake the Meat Wagons
flavor: The horde feeds its columns from a crawling train of carrion wagons. Burn the train and the Carrion Crown's push starves in the field.

title: A Name in the Horde's Court
flavor: Someone close to the Carrion Crown wishes to sell it. The Bone Court will pay for the traitor's name — and pay more if no one learns who bought it.

title: Harvest the Barrow Fields
flavor: Old graves, honored ones, west of {flats}. The Court wants what sleeps there — bones of the named dead, dug and crated. The pay is generous. The Court knows why.

title: Break the Ninth Column
flavor: The horde's Ninth Column masses at the fords. The Court offers war-wages: meet it, break it, and let the Fields drink what's left.

title: Scour the Hollow Crypt
flavor: Revenants of the horde have nested in {crypt}. The Court finds this untidy. Empty it — the Court is indifferent to how.

title: The Push at Mirror Flats
flavor: The Court commits its legions to {flats} for the war's deciding stroke, and buys every sword that can hold a line. Stand with the bone banners, and be remembered by what wins.

title: Cut the Crown's Road
flavor: When the horde breaks, it will run down the Salt Road. The Court pays for the road to be gone when it gets there.

title: The Reliquary Train
flavor: Wagons of grave-goods — crowns, blades, the honored dead's honors — bound for the Court's vaults. See them through the contested ground. Do not ask whose graves.

title: Eyes Inside Bleakgate
flavor: {gate} holds for the horde. The Court wants its walls counted from the inside: gates, wards, watch-turns. Quiet feet and a long memory.

title: Where the Bone Banners March
flavor: A fly-crowned herald speaks for the Carrion Crown: watch the bone legions' roads for a week, and bring back their pattern. The Crown rewards those who count well.

title: Feed the Column
flavor: The horde does not forage; it is fed. Drive the Crown's provision — do not look under the tarps — to the forward camps.

title: Topple the Watch-Spires
flavor: The Bone Court sees too far from its glass spires on the high ground. The Crown would have the Fields dark again.

title: The Herald's Crossing
flavor: The Crown's herald must reach the eastern camps with the Crown's word. The word is heavy; the road is watched. Go around what you cannot go through.

title: Blunt the Bone Sortie
flavor: The Court's legions sally at dawn against the Crown's western camps. The Crown offers living silver for living swords to stand in the gap.

title: Ash the Reagent Barges
flavor: Black-glass reagents float down to the Court's ritualists by barge. The Crown pays for the river to burn.

title: Empty Marrowbrook's Granaries
flavor: The village of {village} hoards grain the Crown requires. Take it. The Crown is indifferent to what the village eats this winter. The pay is very good.

title: Mark the Legate's Path
flavor: A pale legate of the Bone Court travels with a small guard and a large secret. The Crown wants its road, its rests, its hours — everything but the killing, which the Crown keeps for itself.

title: Unseat the Bone Garrison
flavor: A bone legion holds the quarry fort above {ridge}. The Crown wants the fort. It does not want the legion. Resolve the difference.

title: Break the Bridge Shields
flavor: The Court's shield-wall forms at the fords to meet the Ninth Column. The Crown buys hands to break it from the flank while the Column comes on.

title: The Tithe of Meat
flavor: The Crown's carrion wagons crawl to the front under too few guards. Walk beside them. Ignore the smell. Ignore the sounds. Deliver the tithe.

title: Mirror Flats: the Charnel Tide
flavor: The Crown empties its camps onto {flats} for the deciding stroke, and hires every living blade to ride the tide's edge. Stand with the horde, and outlive the argument.

title: Snuff the Last Beacons
flavor: When the Court's line cracks, its beacons will call the reserve legions. The Crown pays for dark hills and unanswered calls.

title: Raise the Fallen Fields
flavor: The battle-dead of {flats} lie wasted in the sun. Cart them to the Crown's raisers. The Crown calls this mercy: everyone marches again.

title: A Key to the Broken Spire
flavor: The Court wards its ritual seat at {spire}. Somewhere a warden's key exists in living hands. The Crown wants the key, the hands, or both.

title: Glasspickers on the Salt Road
flavor: The Glasspickers' Guild moves a season's salvage down {road} and would rather arrive with it. Honest coin for honest walking.

title: Grain Before the Frost
flavor: {village} needs its grain carted in from the far terraces before frost and worse things find it. The village pays in copper and gratitude — mostly gratitude.

title: Clear the Roadside Dead
flavor: Feral dead — no banner, no master — have taken to the culverts along the pilgrim road. The Guild pays by the skull.

title: The Broker's Question
flavor: Ossian the relic-broker slides a purse across the table: 'A caravan vanished on {flats} a month back. Find me the where and the who. The what is my business.'

title: Drive Off the Corpse-Raiders
flavor: Masterless dead have been dragging {village}'s people from their beds. The village elders have pooled what they have. It isn't much. They know it isn't much.

title: The Relic Caravan
flavor: Ossian has bought well and now must move it: three wagons of grave-glass through ground both powers claim. He pays in advance, which tells you what he thinks of the odds.

title: Marrowbrook Calls
flavor: A foraging column of the horde has turned toward {village}. The elders send a boy on a lame horse with everything they can promise. He waits in the yard for an answer.

title: Break the Toll-Camp
flavor: Deserters from six armies have made a toll of the north ford and a sport of the refugees crossing it. The Guild will pay to have the ford opened and the sport ended.

title: Census of the Displaced
flavor: The Guild wants the refugee roads mapped and counted — who moves, where, how many can dig. Cold arithmetic, warm coin.

title: Water for the Mirror Flats
flavor: The wells on {flats} road have gone foul. Cart clean water to the waystations before the crossing kills more than the war does.

title: The Weeping Barrow
flavor: Something old has sat up under the barrow at the pilgrim shrine, and it is calling the lesser dead to it. The shrine-keepers' plate is thin silver, but it is all offered.

title: Flight from Bleakgate
flavor: The war is closing on {gate} and the living inside want out. The Guild has bought passage for three hundred souls. Someone must walk them through a battlefield.

title: Cleanse the Glass Bridge Dead
flavor: Both powers have fed {bridge} for a season and the fallen there no longer stay fallen. The Guild pays for the crossing burned clean — no banners, no questions.

title: Who Wins This War?
flavor: Ossian, quietly: 'Every fool on the Fields has an opinion. I will pay for an answer. Ride the front, count what's true, and tell me which way the glass breaks.'

title: The Last Stand at Marrowbrook
flavor: The war's edge has finally found {village}. The elders no longer offer coin; they offer everything the village will ever have again. The boy on the lame horse is back in the yard.
